former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 創建=(trad.) year 4 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}} | 本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭=Third Sunday of March | 神事=Hadaka Festival | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kawaketa MIkabe
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Kawaketamikabe-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Kawaketamikabe Shrine''' (かわけたみかべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{読み仮名|Jinden-chō|じんでんちょう}}, {{ill|lt=Higashiōmi|Q240891|WD=Q240891|1=Higashiōmi}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a candidate for a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. The shrine crest is Dakisawagata. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Yukaketa-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Seoritsu-hime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} * Inakura-tama-no-Mikoto === History === According to shrine tradition, it was solicited in year 4 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}} by local powerful clan leader Tamao Sukune Isotohiko no Muraji. It is a candidate shrine for the Engishiki {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} "Kawaketa Shrine." In ancient times it was called "Mikawabe Daimyōjin." The present shrine building is said to have been rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 15 ({{ill|lt=1610|Q6765|WD=Q6765|1=1610}}) by Tanaka Yoshihisa, and a ridge tag records "Kaki Misono Mikawabe Shōichii Daimyōjin, Kanzaki District, Gōshū." In early {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was renamed to the current shrine name, and in Meiji 14 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808|1=1881}}) was added as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. [[ファイル:Kanekai Site, gaikan.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Kanekai archaeological site}}]] In {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 20 ({{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|WD=Q2004|1=2008}}), posthole remains believed to be an early Heian three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} honden were found at the nearby Kanekai siteShiga Prefecture cultural properties, historic sites (2012), and one theory infers a relationship with this shrine. === Precinct === ファイル:Kawaketamikabe-jinja, shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings ファイル:Kawaketamikabe-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden ファイル:Kawaketamikabe-jinja, tourou.jpg|Stone lantern (national Important Cultural Property) === Sessha and massha === [[ファイル:Kawaketamikabe-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Precinct shrines}}]] * Daijingū * Kamadokoro Shrine * Hiyoshi Shrine * Kagomori Shrine * Ebisu Shrine === Festivals === * Annual festival - third Sunday of March === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (National designation) === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Stone lantern|1=UNKNOWN}} (craft) - the firebox bears the inscription "Enkyō 2辛亥, 4th month, 18th day." Late {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, made in {{ill|lt=Enkyō (Japan)|Q1327068|WD=Q1327068|lt=Enkyō|1=Enkyō (Kamakura period)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1311|Q5731|WD=Q5731|1=1311}}). Designated on February 2, {{ill|lt=1962|Q2764|WD=Q2764|1=1962}} (Shōwa 37){{国指定文化財等データベース|201|6804|Stone lantern}}. === Transportation === * From {{ill|lt=Yōkaichi Station|Q8062651|WD=Q8062651|1=Yōkaichi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Ohmi Railway Main Line|Q7081104|WD=Q7081104|1=Ohmi Railway Main Line}} and {{ill|lt=Ohmi Railway Yōkaichi Line|Q7081107|WD=Q7081107|1=Ohmi Railway Yōkaichi Line}}, take {{ill|lt=Chokotto Bus|Q11271995|WD=Q11271995|1=Chokotto Bus}}, get off at Mikabe Shrine-mae stop, then a short walk. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === External links === * [https://www.facebook.com/mikabejinjya/ Kawaketa Mikabe Shrine (official Facebook)] * [https://www.higashiomi.net/media/miru/kawaketamikabe Kawaketa Mikabe Shrine (Higashiomi Tourism Association)] {{Commonscat|Kawaketamikabe-jinja}} {{神道 横}}{{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawaketamikabe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawaketamikabe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiomi, Shiga]] [[Category:東近江市の歴史]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:東近江市の重要文化財]]

former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 創建=(trad.) year 4 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|qid=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}} | 本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭=Third Sunday of March | 神事=Hadaka Festival | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kawaketa MIkabe
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Kawaketamikabe-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Kawaketamikabe Shrine''' (かわけたみかべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{読み仮名|Jinden-chō|じんでんちょう}}, {{ill|lt=Higashiōmi|Q240891|qid=Q240891|1=Higashiōmi}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|qid=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a candidate for a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. The shrine crest is Dakisawagata. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Yukaketa-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Seoritsu-hime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} * Inakura-tama-no-Mikoto === History === According to shrine tradition, it was solicited in year 4 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|qid=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}} by local powerful clan leader Tamao Sukune Isotohiko no Muraji. It is a candidate shrine for the Engishiki {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} "Kawaketa Shrine." In ancient times it was called "Mikawabe Daimyōjin." The present shrine building is said to have been rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 15 ({{ill|lt=1610|Q6765|qid=Q6765|1=1610}}) by Tanaka Yoshihisa, and a ridge tag records "Kaki Misono Mikawabe Shōichii Daimyōjin, Kanzaki District, Gōshū." In early {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was renamed to the current shrine name, and in Meiji 14 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|qid=Q7808|1=1881}}) was added as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. [[ファイル:Kanekai Site, gaikan.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Kanekai archaeological site}}]] In {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 20 ({{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|qid=Q2004|1=2008}}), posthole remains believed to be an early Heian three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} honden were found at the nearby Kanekai siteShiga Prefecture cultural properties, historic sites (2012), and one theory infers a relationship with this shrine. === Precinct === ファイル:Kawaketamikabe-jinja, shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings ファイル:Kawaketamikabe-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden ファイル:Kawaketamikabe-jinja, tourou.jpg|Stone lantern (national Important Cultural Property) === Sessha and massha === [[ファイル:Kawaketamikabe-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Precinct shrines}}]] * Daijingū * Kamadokoro Shrine * Hiyoshi Shrine * Kagomori Shrine * Ebisu Shrine === Festivals === * Annual festival - third Sunday of March === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (National designation) === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Stone lantern|1=UNKNOWN}} (craft) - the firebox bears the inscription "Enkyō 2辛亥, 4th month, 18th day." Late {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|qid=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, made in {{ill|lt=Enkyō (Japan)|Q1327068|qid=Q1327068|lt=Enkyō|1=Enkyō (Kamakura period)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1311|Q5731|qid=Q5731|1=1311}}). Designated on February 2, {{ill|lt=1962|Q2764|qid=Q2764|1=1962}} (Shōwa 37){{国指定文化財等データベース|201|6804|Stone lantern}}. === Transportation === * From {{ill|lt=Yōkaichi Station|Q8062651|qid=Q8062651|1=Yōkaichi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Ohmi Railway Main Line|Q7081104|qid=Q7081104|1=Ohmi Railway Main Line}} and {{ill|lt=Ohmi Railway Yōkaichi Line|Q7081107|qid=Q7081107|1=Ohmi Railway Yōkaichi Line}}, take {{ill|lt=Chokotto Bus|Q11271995|qid=Q11271995|1=Chokotto Bus}}, get off at Mikabe Shrine-mae stop, then a short walk. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === External links === * [https://www.facebook.com/mikabejinjya/ Kawaketa Mikabe Shrine (official Facebook)] * [https://www.higashiomi.net/media/miru/kawaketamikabe Kawaketa Mikabe Shrine (Higashiomi Tourism Association)] {{Commonscat|Kawaketamikabe-jinja}} {{神道 横}}{{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawaketamikabe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawaketamikabe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiomi, Shiga]] [[Category:東近江市の歴史]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:東近江市の重要文化財]]
